name: forecasting_compare_sales_forecast_scenarios
description:
Deep-dive forecasting comparison for multiple items. Supports selectors by inventory_ids, sku+country_code, parent_asin, or product_family (pick one). Compares forecast outputs across scenarios and/or forecast runs, with actuals overlay (include_actuals=true by default). Read-only; no side effects.

Latest forecast evaluation:
- The 'actual' scenario/dataset is always excluded when selecting forecast runs
- Actuals are overlaid separately (same periods) when include_actuals=true, enabling actual-vs-forecast comparison

Aggregation:
- query.aggregation.group_by: optional array of dimensions to aggregate by
- Supported: company, brand, product_family, parent_asin, asin
- company_id is always included for authorization
- When aggregated, numeric fields (units_sold, sales_amount) are summed per (group, period, scenario)
- If group_by is omitted, returns per-item detail rows

Company selection:
- Use query.filters.company_id (preferred) or query.filters.company (numeric string)

Actual-vs-forecast comparison:
- Set include_actuals=true (default) and optionally filter scenario_names to compare specific forecasts against actuals
- Output rows have series_type='actual' or series_type='forecast' to distinguish them

filters.description: Item selector and optional filters. Provide exactly one selector path: inventory_ids OR sku + country_code OR parent_asin OR product_family.

company.description: Numeric company ID as string. Deprecated; use company_id (integer) instead. If both provided, company_id takes precedence.

sku.description: Selector path 2: sku array (up to 10). Requires country_code.

sales_channel.description: Filter forecast rows by sales channel (e.g. ['Amazon.com']). Case-insensitive.

aggregation.description: Optional aggregation. If group_by is provided, returns aggregated rows instead of per-item detail.

group_by.description: Dimensions to group by. Returns one row per (group, period, scenario, series_type). company_id is always included. Example: ["brand","product_family"].

limit.description: Max rows returned (each row = one period x scenario x run). If limit exceeded, most recent periods are prioritized.

scenario_ids.description: Optional: scenario id(s) to include. If scenario_ids are provided, scenario_uuids and scenario_names are ignored.

scenario_uuids.description: Optional: scenario uuid(s) to include (e.g., 'manual', 'baseline'). If scenario_ids not provided but scenario_uuids are, scenario_names are ignored.

scenario_names.description: Optional: scenario name(s) to include (exact match recommended). Used only if neither scenario_ids nor scenario_uuids are provided. If none of the three are provided, all available scenarios are included.

run_selector.description: Select which forecast runs (updated_at timestamps) to compare. Defaults to latest_n=3. Used in scenarios/scenarios_and_runs modes; ignored in runs mode (which always selects single scenario's runs).

n.description: When type=latest_n: number of most recent runs to include.

updated_at_from.description: When type=date_range: inclusive lower bound for updated_at.

updated_at_to.description: When type=date_range: exclusive upper bound for updated_at.

include_actuals.description: If true (default), include sales history actuals and align by period.

period.description: Optional period/window selection for the time series output. Periods must be in YYYY-MM format (e.g., 2025-01, 2026-12).

start.description: Start period inclusive (e.g., 2025-01). If omitted, all available past periods included.

end.description: End period inclusive (e.g., 2026-12). If omitted, all available future periods included.

rows.description: Flat result rows. Each row has series_type (actual/forecast), scenario_name, period, units_sold, sales_amount, etc. In aggregated mode, rows also include inventory_count and sku_count.
